note: No option was executed. All require explicit human approval per Lane B mandate (mutation forbidden until authorized).

options.action: Identify and pause whatever is issuing the repeated root SSH sessions from 202.185.89.85 / restarting arifos, before any other remediation
options.evidence: 9 SIGKILLs in ~63min, cadence matching 7 short-lived root SSH sessions from the same source IP in the same window
options.expected_effect: Stops the crash-loop from being actively re-triggered, giving a stable window to diagnose the underlying degraded-boot state without interference
options.validation: Ask Arif directly: 'is 202.185.89.85 you / an agent you're running right now against af-forge?'
options.authority_required: Arif confirmation only — no system change

options.action: Fix DID registry + skills directory permissions (chown/chmod /root/secrets/did/registry.json and /root/AAA/skills/atlas333-cognitive-geometry to match the arifos service user)
options.evidence: Permission denied on both paths on every single boot, blocking challenge issuance and skills wiring
options.expected_effect: Removes two of the four recurring boot-time errors; may reduce (not guaranteed to eliminate) watchdog-perceived unhealthiness
options.blast_radius: low — chown/chmod only, no code or config logic change
options.rollback: revert ownership/mode to prior values (capture with `stat` before changing)
options.validation: re-check journalctl for absence of the two PermissionError lines after next boot
options.authority_required: R2 (change code/config) per AAA/CLAUDE.md — announce + proceed is normally sufficient, but given this is mid-incident, get explicit go-ahead first

options.action: Investigate why register_with_aaa.py gets 503 UNREACHABLE against aaa-a2a even though aaa-a2a answers 200 healthy when curled directly
options.evidence: Every ExecStartPost registration attempt fails with 'fetch failed', independent of AAA's own health
options.expected_effect: Understand whether it's a URL/port mismatch, a startup race (arifos calls before its own network stack is ready), or a timeout too short — informs whether this is cosmetic or a real dependency break
options.blast_radius: none if done by reading register_with_aaa.py and its target URL config only
options.validation: n/a — this is a read step

options.action: Do NOT resolve the uncommitted identity_consistency.py / session_auth.py / tools.py changes in /root/arifOS by reverting or committing without Arif's explicit direction
options.evidence: 18 uncommitted changes to exactly the actor/session subsystem implicated in ACTOR_BINDING_DRIFT — could be an in-progress intentional fix
options.expected_effect: Avoids destroying in-progress work or committing a half-finished refactor
options.blast_radius: n/a (this is a constraint, not an action)
options.validation: Ask Arif what state that work is in before anyone touches it
options.authority_required: Arif — F1 Amanah / reversible-first applies directly here

options.action: Register arif_ops_measure (and any other DENY-by-missing-capability tools) in /contracts/tools.yaml and regenerate the capability graph
options.evidence: KERNEL INTERCEPTOR: DENY ... Capability not registered in graph (v0.2.3), with the fix already suggested in-line by the kernel's own error output
options.expected_effect: Removes one class of DENY responses seen by external callers; does not address the SIGKILL loop itself
options.downtime: requires a kernel restart to pick up regenerated graph — do not restart until the loop itself is stopped
options.validation: arif_kernel_status shows capability present; DENY no longer logged for that tool
options.authority_required: R2, announce + proceed once the crash-loop is stable
